calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-14-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing In Support Of Entry Of Judgment For Monetary Damages Only
Off Calendar Jul-07-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes before the Court for Default Prove-up Hearing in support of Entry of Judgment for monetary damages only.

Dept. 505; Judge Dekreon; Clerk: R. Sahatjian; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 02:24:48 p.m.. and concluding at 2:36:16


Atty. Gaurav S. Bali, Esq. is present via Zoom for Imperial Auto Industries Limited, with witness Vikrim Mehra, Board member of Imperial Auto Industries

Mr. Mehra was placed under oath and testified.

No appearance by Thomas Schwartz.

After reviewing the case file and all the documents presented and confirming there has been no settlement of any kind. The Court confirms that the following:

Damages are $27,600.00
Interest $7, 302.96
Costs $522.00
Attorney Fees $6,500.00
Total $41,924.96

The Court signs the order as modified. The Clerk will email a copy of the signed order to the counsel of the prevailing party.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-18-2023 Continued To Jul-18-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-19-2023 Continued To Apr-18-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2022 Continued To Jan-24-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-08-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for default prove-up hearing.

Dept. 514; Judge Murlene J. Randle; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:10:38 a.m., pausing at 11:25:56 a.m., reconvening at 11:30:57 a.m., and concluding at 11:33:42 a.m.

Plaintiff Imperial Auto Industries Limited represented by Gaurav S. Bali, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Thomas Schwartz not appearing.

Plaintiff presents the facts of the case to the Court.
VIKRAM MEHRA (Company Secretary of Plaintiff and Custodian of Records) is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff under direct examination.

The testimony of Vikram Mehra is interrupted.
Court determines that Plaintiff cannot prove its case. Plaintiff is instructed to comply with 585 Code of Civil Procedure and re-file its request for entry of default judgment consistent with 585 Code of Civil Procedure. Courtesy copies shall be delivered to Department 514.

Plaintiff's request for entry of default is DENIED.
This matter is DROPPED from calendar.
